activities_projects.title: Harvest Festival Research Activity
activities_projects.objective: Explore harvest festivals celebrated in different regions of India
steps: Research a harvest festival celebrated in India such as Pongal, Baisakhi, or Onam
Write about when it is celebrated and why
Describe the customs and traditions followed
Present your findings to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the cultural significance of harvest festivals
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of information and presentation clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Understanding Archaic Words
activities_projects.objective: Develop vocabulary skills by learning archaic words used in poetry
steps: Identify archaic words in the poem such as thou, thee, thy, and thine
Find their modern meanings using a dictionary
Write sentences using the modern forms
Share your sentences with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognise and interpret archaic words in literary texts
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct meanings and sentence formation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary in Context
activities_projects.objective: Understand the meanings of difficult words through context
steps: Read the paragraph containing words like radiance, tribute, cherished, counsel, and munificent
Identify the meaning of each word from the options provided
Explain how the context helped you determine the meaning
Write your answers in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve vocabulary and contextual comprehension

activities_projects.title: Stanza Meaning Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the central idea of each stanza of the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the main idea of each stanza
Match the stanza with the correct theme
Discuss the answers with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret the meaning and themes of the poem
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of stanza ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poetic Devices Identification
activities_projects.objective: Recognise poetic devices used in the poem
steps: Identify examples of imagery, metaphor, alliteration, and personification in the poem
Write the lines that contain these devices
Explain how each device enhances the poem
Share your analysis in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the use of literary devices in poetry
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of identification and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Symbolism in the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Understand symbolic meanings in the poem
steps: Identify symbols such as Surya, Varuna, and Prithvi
Match each symbol with its meaning in the poem
Explain why these natural elements are important for farming
Write your responses in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how symbolism represents natural forces in literature

activities_projects.title: Repetition in Poetry
activities_projects.objective: Understand the purpose of repetition in poetry
steps: Identify repeated phrases in the poem
Write them in your notebook
Discuss why the poet repeats certain phrases
Explain how repetition creates emphasis and rhythm
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how repetition enhances poetic expression
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of explanation and examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Farming Tools Identification
activities_projects.objective: Learn about agricultural tools mentioned in the chapter
steps: Observe the pictures of farming tools
Match the tools with their uses
Complete the names of the implements
Write the answers in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain awareness of traditional farming implements

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion on Nature and Agriculture
activities_projects.objective: Understand the relationship between nature and farming
steps: Form groups of four students
Discuss why farmers depend on the sun, rain, and earth
Relate these ideas to the poem
Present your group’s conclusions to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of natural elements in agriculture
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and quality of discussion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Reflection Writing
activities_projects.objective: Express personal understanding of the poem
steps: Imagine you are a farmer during harvest season
Write a short paragraph thanking nature for the harvest
Include references to the sun, rain, and earth
Share your paragraph with the teacher
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop creative writing and reflective thinking
